title: Framework Agreement for The Provision of External Coaching Services
description:
David MacBrayne Limited (DML) is the parent company of CalMac Ferries Ltd, which in turn is supported by a human resource subsidiary, David MacBrayne HR (UK) Ltd (DMHR).
DMHR are seeking to appoint an experienced supplier to deliver expert Leadership Development Coaching for target groups of staff to ensure their development as leaders and managers is continually supported.

title: Taxi and Private Hire Car Intermediate Course First Aid Awareness Provision
description: The City of Edinburgh Council (the Council) are seeking to establish a contract for a provider to deliver the First Aid Awareness training element of the Taxi and Private Hire Car (PHC) Intermediate Course.

title: Student Assistance and Support Services
description: The Authority is seeking Contractor(s) for the provision of student assistance and support services to the Scottish HE/FE sector (https://www.apuc-scot.ac.uk/?#!/members). These services include: counselling sevices, student support helpline and online mental health and wellbeing support.

title: Maintenance of Building Management System (Hospital and SC)
description: NHS Golden Jubilee Board requires the provision of ongoing maintenance and support of existing Metasys Building Management System within main hospital building and surgical centre.

title: Purchase of Auto Trawl Control System
description: The Scottish Government has a requirement to place a contract with an external service provider for the provision of Auto Trawl Control System. The purchase of the new system will replace an existing system which will require to be swapped out.

title: Dalmuir Park HA Maintenance Framework 2026
description: The successful Candidate(s) will be selected to join any of 17 framework Lots as one of the Providers responsible for reactive & planned maintenance works and services throughout the Associations’ 877 occupied (tenanted and factored) flats/ houses/ closes at Various Addresses, Dalmuir, Clydebank G81.

title: SYMCA - Life Extension for 25 Trams - Supertram Network
description: SYMCA require an organisation to undertake a targeted Life Extension Programme across a fleet of 25 Siemens Trams.  This life extension is required to ensure performance, reliability, structural strength and crashworthiness are maintained at an acceptable level throughout the remaining service life of the fleet to 2035.
